DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-21-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. GEOFFREY BECKER, Esq. of Becker and Becker (tel: 925-939-9041) for Plaintiff Albert Macklin (via Zoom videoconference).
Atty. MICHAEL FOX, Esq. of Duane Morris LLP (tel: 415-957-3000) for Defendant Judicial Council of California.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's May 8, 2024 "opposition to demurrer" does not oppose the demurrer's points. Instead, plaintiff misreads this court's March 19, 2024 order. That order put an earlier demurrer off calendar because the parties had not complied with the meet-and-confer law (CCP 430.41) and provided: "Defendant's response [i.e., demurrer, motion to strike, answer, etc.] is now due by April 19, 2024." The parties subsequently complied with CCP 430.41 and defendant timely filed a new demurrer. Plaintiff says defendant was bound to file the same demurrer as before. Not so. A key purpose of CCP 430.41 is to alter pleadings after a meet-and-confer.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977/ e-mail: sfdepo@veritext.com)(302/RBU)
